Details: Removal of tebuconazole from water by ultraviolet irradiation (UV) can be effective in the presence of both photocatalyst (TiO2) and oxidizing agent [(NH4)2S2O8] at 93% removal versus 10% (UV irradiation alone) and 23% (UV + catalyst only) after 150-minute reaction time in one study [2147]. Doping of the photocatalyst with the transition metal Mo, in turn decreased photocatalytic efficiency under UV light. Less than 80 percent removal was achieved under different dopant concentrations.
